configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH).

field_mapping.description: EchoBird passes model data as 'Attached models for deployment'. Map fields to OpenClaw as follows:
field_mapping.echobird_anthropicUrl: → OpenClaw provider baseUrl (use Option B: anthropic-messages)
field_mapping.echobird_baseUrl: → OpenClaw provider baseUrl (use Option A: openai-completions). If anthropicUrl is present, prefer Option B.
field_mapping.echobird_modelId: → OpenClaw model id AND the primary ref suffix (eb_custom/<modelId>)
field_mapping.echobird_apiKey: → Store in env block as ENV_VAR, reference as ${ENV_VAR} in provider apiKey

steps: 1. Identify which URL to use: if anthropicUrl is present → Option B (anthropic-messages) using anthropicUrl as baseUrl. If only baseUrl → Option A (openai-completions) using baseUrl.
2. Run the merge_script below (fill in the 6 variables at the top from the attached model data).

3. Restart gateway: pkill -f 'openclaw gateway' || true && sleep 1 && export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" && nohup openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured > /tmp/openclaw.log 2>&1 &
configure.after_configure: pkill -f 'openclaw gateway' || true && sleep 1 && export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" && nohup openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured > /tmp/openclaw.log 2>&1 &
